name: German B2 Vocabulary
description: Upper-intermediate German. Complex grammar mostly sorted. Fluent in most situations.

facts.explanation: Abhängigkeit — dependence. Also: dependency, addiction, dependence
Ablauf — process, procedure. Also: expiry, expiration, flow, course
Abreise — departure.
Absage — refusal, rejection. Also: cancellation
Abschnitt — section (cutting). Also: paragraph, segment
Abstand — distance, interspace, spacing, interval, gap.
Akademie — academy (learned society).
Amtssprache — official language.
Anforderung — request. Also: requirement
Anfrage — inquiry.
Angriff — attack, offensive, strike, assault, onslaught, raid, offence.
Anregung — stimulus, excitation. Also: suggestion, proposal
Anspannung — the act or process of tensing, straining. Also: tension
Anstrengung — effort. Also: strenuousness, strain
Anwalt — attorney, lawyer.
Anwendung — application.
Arbeitserlaubnis — work permit.
Arbeitsmarkt — labor market, job market.
Arbeitsvertrag — employment contract.
Armut — poverty.
Aroma — a (generally rather pleasant) spicy or fragrant taste or smell; aroma.
auf — finished; gone (food). Also: up; awake; out of bed
Auffassung — notion, conception, view.
Aufsatz — essay. Also: attachment, add-on (particularly a superficial one)
Auftakt — anacrusis (an unstressed syllable at the start of a verse). Also: anacrusis, kick-off (opening sequence of any event)
Ausdauer — constancy. Also: endurance, perseverance, persistence, stamina
Auseinandersetzung — dealing, examination. Also: conflict, dispute, debate, clash
Ausgang — exit (passage from inside to outside). Also: output, outcome
Ausnahme — exception. Also: exception (interruption in normal processing, typically caused by an error condition)
Ausstattung — equipment, facility. Also: fittings, furnishings, scenography, scenery and costumes, decor
Aussteller — exhibitor. Also: drawer (of a cheque), issuer
aussterben — to die out, become extinct; (figurative) to cease (being used, practiced, etc.).
Ausweis — ID card (a card or badge showing the official identity of the bearer).
Auswirkung — implication, effect, consequence, ramification, repercussion.
Auszeit — time-out. Also: time-out, hiatus
Auszug — moveout (the act of moving out of a property). Also: extract, excerpt
Außenminister — foreign minister (male or of unspecified gender).
Bachelor — bachelor's degree. Also: bachelor graduate
Backware — pastries, baked goods.
Bakterium — bacterium.
Ballett — ballet.
Barriere — barrier.
Baumwolle — cotton (plant). Also: cotton (thread or fabric made from said plant)
Bauwerk — building, edifice.
Beachtung — regard, heed, attention. Also: observance, observation, notice
bearbeiten — to edit. Also: to work on something, to process (documents, forms, etc.), to take something to, to hit, to strike (repeatedly)
Bedarf — need (that which is desired or required). Also: demand (amount of input that is required for something to function)
Bedürfnis — desire, urge. Also: need, necessity
Befragung — interrogation (act of interrogating or questioning).
Begleiter — companion. Also: escort, accompanist, article
Begründung — reason, rationale, grounds. Also: explanation, justification
Belastung — load, weight, pressure, strain. Also: burden
Beliebtheit — popularity.
Beobachtung — monitoring; surveillance; supervision. Also: observation, sighting (of a bird, animal, UFO, etc.)
Beschränkung — restriction.

Beschwerde — complaint (a grievance, problem, difficulty, or concern; the act of complaining). Also: (bodily) complaints, discomforts, (medical) physical trouble, (medical) symptoms, physical illness, aches and pains, infirmities
Besonderheit — feature.
Betracht — consideration.
Betrachter — beholder, observer, viewer.
Betrag — amount, sum. Also: the magnitude or length of a vector
Betriebsrat — works council.

Bewertung — evaluation, rating. Also: expert opinion, assessment (in a professional capacity), judging (for example, of entries in a contest), assessment (act of assessing a tax), appraisal, valuation (as for sale, assessment, or taxation), review (i.e., customer's report)
Bezeichnung — designation, denomination, name, term. Also: denotation, representation
Bezug — reference. Also: cover (e.g. of a cushion or couch), salary (of a civil servant), subscription, relationship
Bibel — Bible, bible.
Blitz — a bolt of lightning. Also: flash
Blutdruck — blood pressure.
Bluthochdruck — hypertension, high blood pressure.
Buchdruck — letterpress.
Bundesagentur — federal agency.

Bundesbürger — a citizen of the Federal Republic of Germany, whether they live in Germany or not (male or of unspecified gender). Also: someone who lives in the Federal Republic of Germany, whether they are citizens or not (male or of unspecified gender)
Bundesregierung — federal government.
böse — evil; malicious; wicked. Also: bad; naughty, mean, dark (of jokes, satire, tricks, etc.), angry
Büchse — box, can. Also: rifle, bushing
Bügeleisen — iron (for ironing clothes).
Bürgerinitiative — citizen action group.
Charakter — character; the totality of someone's personal qualities. Also: character, characteristic feature or nature of a thing, place, or people, character, symbol, written glyph, title, rank
Christus — Christ (Jesus seen as the messiah).
Couch — sofa, couch.
Demonstrant — demonstrator, protester (male or of unspecified gender).
Diakonie — (professional) social work (social welfare work) provided by churches.
Dichter — poet (male or of unspecified gender).
Diebstahl — theft.
Diplom — diploma (higher education only, e.g., Master’s degree)..
Diskriminierung — discrimination.
Disziplin — discipline.
Dorn — thorn (with plural Dornen or Dörner). Also: clipping of (with plural Dorne) Dornbusch (“thornbush”), bolt (with plural Dorne)

Dschungel — jungle. Also: jungle, thicket, a place where things or instruments mingle to an extent that effort is required for an outsider to acquire an overview., jumble, tangle, plethora (a large amount of options or concepts that is not very intuitive or easy to distinguish between)
Durchbruch — breakthrough (sudden progress overcoming an obstacle). Also: aperture, cut-out, openwork (in a workpiece)
Durchhaltevermögen — stamina.
Effekt — effect.
Ehrenamt — volunteer work.
Einblick — insight.
Einfühlungsvermögen — empathy (capacity to understand another person's point of view).
Einschaltquote — viewership count.
Einschränkung — constraint, restraint.
Einschätzung — assessment, estimation.
Einzelhandel — retail (sale of goods directly to the consumer). Also: monopoly
Empfindung — sensation. Also: feeling, emotion
Englischkenntnis — English skills.
Entdeckung — discovery.
Entspannung — relaxation. Also: recreation, catharsis, detente
Enttäuschung — disappointment, let-down, bummer, frustration. Also: disenchantment, disillusionment, disillusion, anticlimax
Entwurf — draft. Also: design, model, sketch
erachten — to consider. Also: to deem
Erfinder — inventor (male or of unspecified gender).
Erfindung — invention. Also: fabrication
Erkrankung — the act or process of falling ill. Also: illness; disease
Erlaubnis — permission (authorisation).
erlernen — to learn (to the point of being an expert).
Ermittler — investigator, detective.
Ernst — earnestness, seriousness. Also: a serious statement, a seriously meant action
Ernte — harvest.
Erstellung — creation. Also: preparation, generation, building, construction
Eröffnung — opening.
Examen — exam (particularly at the end of university studies e.g. Final Exam).
Extremsport — extreme sport.
Fach — compartment. Also: drawer, subject
Falle — trap, snare. Also: spring latch, bed
Fallschirm — parachute.
Fee — fairy.
Feind — enemy, fiend, foe (male or of unspecified gender).
Fertigkeit — skill, proficiency, facility, dexterity.
Fluggast — air passenger.
Fluggesellschaft — airline (company that flies airplanes).
Football — the kind of ball used in American football. Also: the game of American football
Forderung — a demand, a (financial) claim. Also: call (i.e., decision made publicly)
Formulierung — wording, phrasing. Also: formulation
Fotograf — photographer.
Freundlichkeit — kindness, friendliness (the state of being kind). Also: kindness (an act of kindness)
Frucht — fruit (seed-bearing part of a plant). Also: harvest, crop, produce, particularly of cereal, result, effect, fruit

Förderung — support, promotion, advancement (financial, moral, or emotional advancement).. Also: extraction, mining (of natural resources) (no plural)., transport, conveyance (movement of solid, liquid, or gaseous materials).
Führungsposition — leading position.
Fürst — prince (sovereign prince).
Geburtsdatum — date of birth.
Gefängnis — prison, jail (building). Also: prison sentence
Gegenwart — the present. Also: presence (of someone/something)
Gegner — adversary, opponent, enemy.
Geheimnis — secret. Also: mystery
Gehirn — brain.
Geist — spirit. Also: the Holy Spirit; Holy Ghost, essence, mind, wit, ghost; spook, spook
Geldbeutel — wallet, purse.
Gemeinsamkeit — common ground. Also: commonality, commonness, similarity, thing in common, common feature, shared characteristic
Gemälde — painting.
Gemüsesorte — kind, type of vegetable.
Genie — genius.
Germanistik — German studies (German language, literature, and culture). Also: Germanic studies (Germanic languages, literatures, and cultures)
Gespür — intuition.
Gestaltung — design, styling, arrangement.
Geste — gesture.
Gestik — gestures (as a motion of the limbs or body, especially one made to emphasize speech).
Getreide — cereal; corn; grain (type of plant and its fruit). Also: a particular kind of cereal
Gewerkschaft — labor union, trade union.
Gewohnheit — habit.
Gift — poison; toxin; venom.
Gleiche — equality. Also: evenness, topping out (the finishing of a building's roof)
Globalisierung — globalisation.
Glocke — bell (percussive instrument).
Grashalm — blade of grass.
Griff — grasp, reach (act of trying to grab). Also: grip (act of holding something after having gripped it), handle (part of a tool or fixture meant to be gripped), hold
Großteil — major part, majority.
Grundrecht — basic right, fundamental right.
Halbpension — half board.
Halt — hold; adhesion. Also: foothold; grip, support, stop (e.g. of a train), halt; cessation
Haltung — posture (manner of holding one's body). Also: attention, attitude, stance, keeping, husbandry (now especially of animals)
Handelsbeziehung — trade relationship, commercial relation (the ongoing exchange of goods, services, and capital between two parties, such as companies or nations).
Handwerk — labour, especially of a manual type. Also: trade (skilled practice), something made by hand, handcraft
Hauptsatz — main clause (a clause that can stand alone syntactically). Also: fundamental theorem
Hauptschule — a type of basic secondary school in Germany that follows elementary school but does not qualify for higher university education.
Hausordnung — house regulations.
Herberge — hostel, inn (accommodation for travellers, often serving breakfast and cold supper, but typically no hot food). Also: home, shelter, place to sleep
Herzinfarkt — myocardial infarction (necrosis of heart muscle), heart attack.
Hierarchie — hierarchy (body of ruling officials).
Hilfsorganisation — aid agency.
Hinsicht — regard, aspect, respect, viewpoint. Also: concern
Hochstapler — conman, con man, impostor.
Hochwasser — a very high water level in any body of water; high tide; high water; in the case of a river often, but not necessarily, implying flooding.
Hotelgast — hotel guest.

Hürde — hurdle. Also: obstacle for athletes, any obstacle to be overcome, mobile fencing element (formerly often of twigs, now more often other materials), rack on which fruit and vegetables are stored or dried
Individualität — individuality.
Infrastruktur — infrastructure.
Investition — investment (a placement of capital in expectation of deriving income or profit from its use).
Irrtum — error, mistake.
Kampagne — campaign.
Kampf — struggle, battle, campaign, fight.
Kanal — canal (man-made watercourse). Also: something that resembles such a watercourse, e.g. a vessel in the body, channel (networks through which pass information, influence, etc.), channel
Kanton — canton (state of Switzerland). Also: canton, canton (military enrollment district of Prussia, made obsolete by introduction of universal conscription in 1813), township (of Canada)
kapital — magnificent, marvelous to look at. Also: significant, important, archetypical
Katalog — catalogue (systematic list of names, books, pictures, etc.; complete list of items; university calendar).
Kaution — bail. Also: caution, security deposit, damage deposit
Keks — cookie (US), biscuit (UK).
kennenlernen — to get to know, become acquainted with (a person, place, thing); to befriend.. Also: to meet; introduce oneself to; to present.
Kindererziehung — parenting.
Kirsche — cherry. Also: a ball, especially a football, bullet
Klang — sound, tone. Also: tune, melody, reputation
Klassenzimmer — classroom.
Klassiker — classic (an author or work of lasting import, a part of the canon of a genre or field). Also: classic (a joke, situation or occurrence that is considered well-known, typical or relatable)
Kleiderschrank — wardrobe (cabinet in which clothes may be stored).
klettern — to climb (e.g. up a tree).
Kloster — convent, monastery, cloister.
Kollektion — collection, set of items.
Kommilitone — fellow student, classmate (at college or university), uni mate, schoolmate (male or of unspecified gender).
Kommissar — commissioner. Also: title of a police officer at the beginning of the upper service
Konstruktion — construction.
Kontrast — contrast.
Konzentration — concentration (all senses).
Korrektur — correction.
Korruption — corruption.
Kraut — herb; useful plant (plant used to flavour food, or for medicinal effect). Also: herbaceous plant, cabbage (vegetable), a thick syrup made from sugar beets or, less often, fruit
Kreativität — creativity.
Kreuz — cross. Also: clubs, sharp sign, ♯, lower back
Kriminalität — crime (practice or habit of committing crimes; criminal acts collectively). Also: criminality, criminalness (state of being criminal)
Kriterium — criterion.
Kritiker — critic.
Kuriosität — curiosity.
Landessprache — national language.
lange — long, for a long time. Also: in a long time
Last — load, burden.
Lauf — run, race. Also: leg of certain animals, barrel, course (onward movement), course, way
Lausitz — Lusatia (a traditionally Slavic-speaking cultural region in Central Europe, in modern Germany, Poland and the Czech Republic).
Lebensbedingung — condition of life.
Lebensbereich — area of life.
Lebenserwartung — life expectancy, lifespan (of organism).
Lebensgefährte — significant other, life partner, life companion, companion.
Lebensstil — lifestyle.
Lebensweise — lifestyle, way of life.
Lehrkraft — professional teacher (at any level from primary school to university).
Leistungsfähigkeit — efficiency, effectiveness, performance, capability, ability.
Leseratte — bookworm (avid reader).
Lieferant — supplier, provider, deliverer.
Lippe — lip.
Lücke — gap, lacuna.
Mangel — lack, shortage (the condition of having an insufficient amount of a resource, or none at all). Also: dietary deficiency
Masse — mass (quantity of matter). Also: matter, ground
Master — master's degree. Also: master graduate
Maßnahme — measure (tactic or strategy). Also: sanction, scheme, activity, method, step, action, device
Maßstab — measuring rod, yardstick, rule. Also: scale (of a map, model), measure, standard, criterion, yardstick
Mediziner — doctor, physician (male or of unspecified gender).
Meeting — a business meeting; a scheduled formal meeting for a specific purpose or with an agenda.
Mehrheit — majority; bulk. Also: majority (specified as absolute Mehrheit), plurality (specified as relative Mehrheit)
Mehrsprachigkeit — multilingualism.
Meinungsäußerung — expression of opinion.
Mietwohnung — rented apartment.
Milieu — atmosphere, ambiance, environment, setting (in general). Also: a specific social environment or setting, underworld; demimonde; world of crime, drugs, and/or prostitution
Mimik — facial expressions.
Ministerium — ministry.
Ministerpräsident — minister-president, prime minister.
Mischung — mix, mixture. Also: shuffle
Mitarbeit — cooperation, collaboration. Also: assistance
Mittagsschlaf — afternoon nap.
Mittelfeld — midfield. Also: the middle, the mediocre area of a range or spectrum, the middle part in the Feldermodell
Mobbing — bullying. Also: mobbing
Moderne — modernity. Also: modernism, the modern era
Mord — murder.
Nachfolger — successor.
Nachhaltigkeit — sustainability.
Nachname — last name, surname.
Nachrichtensendung — news program, newscast.

Nachwuchs — the act of regrowing. Also: that which regrows; regrowth, offspring, progeny (collective of biological descendants), juniors, new blood (collectively), youngsters, next generation (the collective of young people that will supersede the current generation (in a particular domain))
Nahrung — nourishment, food.
Nationalsozialismus — National Socialism.
Naturschutz — conservation.
Naturwissenschaft — natural science.
Nebenjob — Side job..
Nervenkitzel — thrill.
Neugier — curiosity.
Neuigkeit — news (item of).
Nickerchen — nap, catnap, snooze.
Nobelpreis — Nobel Prize.
Notebook — notebook computer.
Notwendigkeit — necessity.
Nutzer — user.
Oberschicht — upper class.
Objektivität — objectivity, impartiality.
Opfer — sacrifice. Also: victim, loser, wimp (someone weak or dependent; one who cannot stand up for themselves)
Partnersuche — dating, searching for a partner.
Passwort — password.
perfekt — perfect.
Philosophie — philosophy (study of thought). Also: name of the school subject taken by pupils who choose not to attend religious education, philosophy (one's manner of thinking)
Physiker — physicist. Also: physician
Phänomen — phenomenon.
Portemonnaie — wallet, purse.
Porträt — portrait.
Prinzessin — princess (female member of a royal family other than a queen, especially a daughter or granddaughter).
Priorität — priority.
Privatperson — private individual.
Produzent — producer.
Provokation — provocation.
Psychologe — psychologist (male or of unspecified gender).
Quelle — spring. Also: well (oil, gas), source
Quote — a quota. Also: share, proportion
Ratte — rat.
rauchen — to smoke, give off smoke. Also: to smoke (tobacco etc.)
Realität — reality (state of being real). Also: reality, fact (that which is real), dasein, real estate
Recherche — research, investigation (many contexts, but especially journalism).
Rechtfertigung — justification.
Rechtsanwalt — lawyer.
Redakteur — editor (male or of unspecified gender).

Reich — the Holy Roman Empire, the First Reich. Also: the Deutsche Reich, the (Deutsche) Kaiserreich, the Second Reich (1871–1918), the Weimar Republic (official name, 1919–1933), the Third Reich (1933–1945)
Relativitätstheorie — theory of relativity.

Reportage — a report of some length and depth, including first-hand accounts, sometimes investigative. Also: a documentary about a current issue; usually implying that the issue is, or should be, of interest to politics and general society (which Dokumentation does not necessarily imply), live coverage with commentary
Roboter — robot.
Rohstoff — raw material, material. Also: resource, commodity
Rote — female Red (communist or socialist).
Rubrik — column (of newspaper). Also: category, rubric
Ruf — call. Also: reputation
Rundfunk — broadcasting (The business or profession of radio and television.). Also: radio (technical means)
Rätsel — riddle, puzzle, mystery, enigma.
Sachverhalt — circumstance (surrounding the facts), factual findings.
Schatten — shade, shadow.
Scheidung — separation. Also: divorce
scheitern — to fail, to conclude unsuccessfully. Also: to flounder
Schicht — layer, stratum, seam. Also: sheet, ply, coat (e.g. of paint), class, stratum, group of people with a certain social status, shift (day's work period), stint
Schlafmangel — sleep deficit (lack of sleep).
Schlafsack — sleeping bag (padded or insulated bag).
Schlafstörung — sleep disorder.
Schlag — blow, hit. Also: beat (pulse on the beat level)
Schlaganfall — apoplexy, stroke.
Schriftsprache — written language. Also: standard language
Schuss — shot. Also: ammunition, (mild) craziness, (sexually) attractive person, usually female, narcotic injection, schuss (straight run downhill), dash (small quantity of a liquid)
Schutz — protection. Also: protective installation
Schwiegermutter — mother-in-law.
Schädling — pest.
Schätzung — estimate, estimation, guess. Also: appraisal, assessment, valuation
Seil — rope, line, cord.

sein — to be. Also: to feel (to experience a condition), to feel like, to be in the mood for, forms the present perfect and past perfect tenses of certain intransitive verbs, to exist; there to be; to be alive, to have the next turn (in a game, in a queue, etc.), to be "it"; to be the tagger in a game of tag
Selbstbewusstsein — aplomb, self-assurance, self-confidence. Also: self-awareness
Seltenheit — rarity. Also: scarcity, curiosity
Sicherung — fuse (device preventing overloading of a circuit). Also: securing, safeguarding
Sicht — view. Also: sight, visibility, point of view, aspect
Sorbe — Sorb.
Soziologe — sociologist (male or of unspecified gender).
Spannung — voltage. Also: tension, suspense (emotion of anticipation, excitement, anxiety or apprehension)
Spezialist — specialist.
Spielzeug — toy. Also: toys
Spitze — cusp. Also: point, tip, peak, top, front, lead, spike, lace (textile pattern), striker, leadership, a sarcastic remark, a jab
Spitzenreiter — front runner, leader. Also: bestseller; chart topper
Sportler — sportsman, athlete.
Sportveranstaltung — sporting event.
Sprachgebrauch — language use, usage; linguistic usage.

Spruch — short saying, expression or dictum that is remembered or is meaningful in itself. Also: stock phrase, slogan, sharp remark or repartee, witticism, witty quote, proverb, saying, popular verse or short poem, particular kind of medieval lyric poem, judgment, decision
Spurensicherung — securing of evidence. Also: forensic squad, forensic department
Staatsbürger — citizen (legal member of a state).
Stadtrat — city council.
Stamm — tree trunk, main stem of a plant (but never used of thin stalks or twigs). Also: tribe, stem (of a word), phylum, strain (of bacteria, etc.)
Standort — location, place, site. Also: base, position
Stellenwert — position, status (value that depends upon position). Also: place value
Stellung — position. Also: sex position, posing; lodging, medical examination
Stellungnahme — statement, comment, expression of an opinion. Also: opinion, view on a particular subject
sterben — to die.
Stiftung — foundation.
Stil — style.
Stille — stillness, silence.
Strafanzeige — criminal charge.
Straßenverkehr — road traffic.
Strömung — current, stream, flux, flow. Also: movement (in politics, ideas, the arts, etc.)
Studiengang — major, academic course.

Stufe — step, tread (of a stair or ladder). Also: degree, stage, level, rank, phase, clipping of Jahrgangsstufe: year (all pupils of a year or grade, regardless of forms), an aggregation of standalone and well-crystallized mineral, age
Subjekt — subject. Also: person
Tagesordnung — agenda; order of the day (business to be done by a body or group of people on a particular day).
Taschengeld — pocket money.
Taste — key, button (on a piano, keyboard, etc.).
Tat — deed. Also: act, action, crime, offense
Tatsache — fact (a true observation).
Telefongespräch — telephone conversation, conversation had by telephone.
Tempo — tempo, pace (rate, speed).
Tendenz — tendency.
Tennisplatz — tennis court.
Textilie — textile(s).
Theorie — theory.
Tierschutz — animal protection / welfare.
Tierwelt — animal kingdom.
Tischler — joiner (wooden furniture manufacturer).
Toleranz — tolerance.
Torwart — goalkeeper, goal keeper.
tropfen — to drop, drip. Also: to leak (said of a faucet)
Umfeld — environment (political or social setting, arena or condition).
Umsatz — turnover, revenue, sales. Also: conversion
Umsetzung — implementation, execution, realisation.
Umstand — circumstance (an event; a fact; a particular incident). Also: fuss, trouble, bother (unnecessary activity)
Unterbrechung — interruption. Also: interrupt
Unwetter — severe weather, storm, rainstorm (an instance of weather characterized by strong wind and/or rainfall).
Urteil — judgment (the preferred spelling in a legal context), verdict, sentence (court decision). Also: judgement, opinion, usually one given after detailed consideration
Verabschiedung — dismissal. Also: seeing off, enactment, passage, adoption, funeral service, especially a Lutheran one
Verband — bandage. Also: association, union (kind of organization), unit, lattice
Verbesserung — improvement. Also: correction
Verbrechen — a felony, indictable offence. Also: a crime; any criminal offence, crime (illegal activity as such, at large; criminals collectively)
Verbrecher — criminal.
Verbreitung — spreading, dissemination. Also: sale, selling, distribution, spread, distribution, dispersal, propagation
Verbrennung — burning, combustion. Also: burn
Vereinigung — unification (act of unifying), joining. Also: trade union, unity
Verfassung — constitution. Also: condition, state, writing, composition, drafting

verlassen — to leave, to abandon; to depart, to forsake. Also: to leave, to desert, to dump (e.g. a partner in a romantic relationship), to pass away; to cease; to die (with direct object uns), to exit or close a computer program or app, to trust; to rely on
Verlängerung — prolongation; lengthening (process of making something longer, in space or duration). Also: lengthening (process of becoming longer, in space or duration), extra time, overtime
Verordnung — regulation, ordinance, statutory instrument.
Verpackung — packaging (the materials used to pack something). Also: process of packaging, or its result
Versorgung — supply. Also: care, treatment, accommodation
Vertrieb — sale, sales distribution, marketing, trafficking.
Verzicht — abstention. Also: renunciation, relinquishment, waiver, sacrifice
Veränderung — change (process of becoming different).
Vielzahl — multitude.
Visum — visa (permit to enter a country). Also: visa (stamp on a passport)
Vogelhochzeit — Birds’ Wedding.
Vokabel — word (vocabulary word, item of vocabulary), vocable.

Volk — people, nation, folk, tribe, race (group united by culture, history, descent, and/or language). Also: people, population, citizens, common people, the lower classes, the working classes, folk, crowd (large group of people gathered somewhere), herd, covey, swarm, colony; chiefly of insects
Vorjahr — previous year. Also: synonym of Frühjahr (“spring”)

Vorlage — source material, original, inspiration (the material that is adapted into a piece of media or art, e.g. a novel turned into a film, a historical person transformed into a fictional character, or a photo copied into a pencil sketch). Also: template (generic layout or form to be specified with concrete data), presentation, submission (of a document requested/required as part of a process), setup, assist, opportunity (something which is promptly referenced by someone else to make a joke, argument, segue or criticism), assist (decisive pass that leads to a goal or similar successful play), forward-leaning posture, vorlage, draft, document (for evaluation or ratification by a wider circle)
Vorlesung — lecture.
Vorwurf — reproach. Also: accusation
Wahrheit — truth.
Wandel — transformation, change. Also: lifestyle; way of life, defect
Warnstreik — token strike, warning strike.
Wartezeit — waiting time.
Web — the Web; the World Wide Web.
Wechsel — change. Also: bill of exchange, ellipsis of Wechseljahre n pl; menopause

Wende — turn (change in temperament or circumstance). Also: turnaround (reversal of policy), tacking, the process that opened the way to the unification of West and East Germany; that period (1989–90) in general
Wendung — turn. Also: expression, phrase
Wette — bet.

Widerstand — resistance (act of resisting, or the capacity to resist). Also: resistance (physics: force that tends to oppose motion), resistance (physics: opposition of a body to the flow of current), resistance (sociology/history: An underground organization engaged in a struggle for liberation from forceful occupation), resistor (electronic component)
Wohlstand — prosperity. Also: affluence, wealth
Wurf — throw. Also: litter (of cubs)
Wärme — warmth. Also: heat
Zahnarzt — dentist.
Zeitalter — age; era (historic period of time).
Zeitplan — timetable, schedule.
Zeitreise — time travel.
Zeitungsartikel — newspaper article.
Zertifikat — certificate.
Zielgruppe — target group, target audience.
Zitat — quotation.
Zufriedenheit — contentment, satisfaction.
Zugang — access. Also: admission, intake, entrance
Zusage — commitment, pledge, undertaking. Also: covenant (binding agreement)
Zusammenfassung — summary, compilation, summing up, recapitulation. Also: abstract, synopsis, résumé, précis
Zusammenhang — coherence, cohesion. Also: interrelation, correlation, connection, connectivity, relation, nexus, link, context
Zusammenschluss — union, federation, merger, amalgamation, alliance.
Zustimmung — agreement (expression or indication that an agreement has been reached). Also: approval, consent
Zweig — branch, twig. Also: branch; section
abbauen — to dismantle, disassemble. Also: to mine (an ore), to degrade, metabolise, to relieve, reduce (e.g. stress)
abgrenzen — to delimit, to demarcate.
abreißen — to tear off. Also: to tear down, to demolish
abschalten — to switch off. Also: to unwind, relax
abweichen — to differ or vary. Also: to deviate or diverge
afrikanisch — African.
Aktiv — active voice (the form in which the subject of a verb carries out some action). Also: active verb
akut — acute, sudden. Also: urgent, immediate
allgemein — general (common, basic, usual), universal.
alltäglich — everyday. Also: quotidian, usual, common, trivial
alternativ — alternative.
andererseits — on the other hand.
anfertigen — to manufacture, to make, to produce, to create, to craft; to draw up (a document).

anhalten — to stop (cease moving). Also: to continue, to last, to stop, to hold, to keep from moving, to urge, to encourage, to compel (to do something) (often by referring to the matter repeatedly), to hold tight, to hold on to something or someone, to ask for

anlegen — to create. Also: to put on (clothing, jewelry, etc.), to strap on, to start a fight, to lay in, set aside (a supply, a reserve, a stock), to save (money, capital) with interest, to invest, to apply (criteria, standards), to lay out, to arrange, to design (a park, garden, etc.), to be determined (to), to aim, to dock
anpassen — to adapt, to adjust. Also: to adapt to, to adjust to

ansiedeln — to cause to live or be based in a place (e.g. people, businesses, wild animals); to settle; to locate; to establish; to introduce. Also: to set up a home or base in a place; to settle down; to locate

antreten — to arrive; to present oneself. Also: to line up; to stand for a muster or report, to compete; to run, to begin (a duty or task, also a voyage); to take (office); to accept (an inheritance), to kick against (a device or machine) to start it; to kick start (a motorcycle), to tread down (soil)
anweisen — to instruct. Also: to direct, order

anzeigen — to display, to show (a measurement e.g. time, temperature, speed etc.). Also: to indicate, to announce (a marriage, engagement, change of premises etc.); to advertise, to report (to the police)
arbeitend — working.
auffordern — to ask, request. Also: to invite (someone to dance, etc.)
aufgreifen — to capture, apprehend, seize. Also: to continue, to take up
auflösen — to break up, dissipate, sever. Also: to dissolve, to resolve, to dematerialize
ausbauen — to remove a part from a machine or device. Also: to extend, to rebuild something into something else, to serve another purpose
ausbilden — to educate; to train; to instruct (for a job, skill). Also: to develop; to shape; to design
ausbrechen — to break out.

ausführen — to take out, to take or lead outside. Also: to take (a pet, especially a dog) for a walk, to take (people, especially a woman) to a restaurant, on a date etc., to export (sell to another country), to cause to proceed, to carry out, execute, perform, realize, to execute, run, to treat or explain in detail, to elaborate on
ausführlich — detailed, elaborate.
ausgeschrieben — written out.
ausreisen — to depart (from a country), to exit (a country).

ausrichten — to align. Also: to justify or orient, to rectify; to achieve (something by changing a situation), to pass on (a message), to badmouth; to make negative comments about (someone), to pay out (a pension etc.)
ausstatten — to equip. Also: to endow, provide, furnish, stock

austragen — to (go from house to house and) deliver sth.,. Also: to take someone or something off a list, to be in or have a struggle for victory with sb. (in any competitive event with another opposing group or person, e.g. a war, a fight, a battle, a duel, a championship, a contest, a match, a (scientifical) debate, a discussion, an argument, an (internal) conflict), to stage, to hold, to execute, to host such a competitive event,, to bear a child until birth, to gestate, to insist on (as a condition of something)
auswerten — to evaluate, assess. Also: to analyze, to appreciate
auszeichnen — to distinguish (make different). Also: to honour, award, decorate
automatisch — automatic.
außergewöhnlich — exceptional, extraordinary.
basieren — to base. Also: to be based
beauftragen — to mandate, to order from, to hire for a particular business.
bedecken — to cover. Also: to make mate
bedenken — to consider, bethink. Also: to keep in mind, to remember (someone) in one's will; to gift (someone) with; to treat (someone) to, to think; consider (before making a decision)
bedeutend — significant, meaningful.
bedrohen — to threaten, menace.
befragen — to question, to interview, to interrogate.
befreunden — to befriend, to become friends with, to make friends with.
befördern — to move; to convey; to transport; to carry. Also: to promote, to raise (an employee's, soldier's, etc.) responsibility or rank, to promote (a development)
befürworten — to endorse, to support, to back, to advocate.
begegnen — meet, to encounter, to come across. Also: to face, to confront, to counter, to respond to, to handle
begehen — to commit, perpetrate (a crime, an offense). Also: to walk, to walk on, to use, to visit, to inspect, to celebrate
begeistert — enthusiastic, thrilled. Also: delighted
begraben — to bury (also figuratively).
begrenzt — confined, limited, restricted. Also: narrow
beibringen — to teach. Also: to break (news, information) carefully to, to inflict on, inflict with, to bring, to provide as evidence
beinhalten — to comprise, to contain, to include, to involve. Also: to comprise, to entail, to imply, to implicate
beitragen — to contribute to (to give something, that is or becomes part of a larger whole).
beklagen — to pity, to lament. Also: to complain
belastbar — resilient, reliable. Also: loadable

belegen — to cover. Also: to fill (a sandwich etc.), to document, back, substantiate, to enroll for; to take (a course), to bombard, to reserve (a seat, room etc.); to occupy (a building), to mate with (a female animal)
bemerkenswert — remarkable, noteworthy.
bemühen — to make an effort. Also: to trouble oneself
beruhen — to consist. Also: to depend, to be based
beruhigen — to calm down, to quiet. Also: to become calm
berücksichtigen — to consider, regard, take into account or consideration.
bestrafen — to punish.
bestätigen — to confirm, to corroborate.
beteiligen — to let someone take part in something. Also: participate
betreiben — to run, to operate (a business, an engine, etc.). Also: to do (sport, trade), to pursue (a policy, a goal), to pursue (someone) in order to collect a debt; to dun
betroffen — shocked. Also: affected, concerned
beurteilen — to form an opinion on, to judge.
bevorzugen — to favor, to prefer.
beweglich — nimble, agile. Also: versatile, moveable, flexible, mobile, moving
beweisen — to prove. Also: to display, to show, to demonstrate, to make hay
bewusst — conscious, aware. Also: intentional
bewähren — to show (e.g. courage). Also: to prove oneself

binden — to bind, to tie, to fasten something by means of a string etc.. Also: to tie, to fasten a string etc., to oblige, commit, bind, to commit oneself, make a commitment (especially for marriage), to make congeal, thicken, set, bond, to capture; to absorb (carbon dioxide etc.), to congeal, thicken, set, bond, to make sandhi, crasis, liaison
bisherig — previous, so far.
bislang — so far, hitherto, until now.
blättern — to browse. Also: to peel off, to flake off
bundesweit — nationwide.
checken — to check (up), to inspect, examine. Also: to grasp, to understand, to get, to procure, to obtain, to gain, to body check
damalig — then, at the time.
darunter — below it, below that, thereunder. Also: among these, including (indicates membership in a mentioned set)
dazwischen — between them. Also: between, in between
definieren — to define.
derzeit — currently, nowadays. Also: back then
detailliert — detailed.
deuten — to interpret (e.g. dreams). Also: to point
deutlich — articulate, clear. Also: distinct, considerable
dicht — thick, tight, dense. Also: impermeable, sealed, shut, locked (preventing passage or entrance), closed, out of operation, out of business, close, tight, intoxicated
dokumentieren — to document.
doppelt — double (made up of two matching or complementary elements).
dreijährig — three-year-old.
drängen — to press, to push.
durchaus — absolutely, fully, entirely. Also: absolutely, definitely, certainly, in any case
durchführen — to perform, to conduct (a study, a ceremony, an operation, etc.). Also: to implement, to carry out, to execute, to put into action (a decision, measures, etc.)
durchlesen — to read through. Also: to peruse
duzen — to address informally using the pronoun du, to thou. Also: to be on first-name terms
dynamisch — dynamic.
effektiv — effective. Also: actual
einatmen — to inhale, breathe in.
einerseits — on the one hand.
einfangen — to capture, to catch. Also: to catch, to be infected with (a disease, an illness)
einheimisch — domestic (internal to a specific country).
einheitlich — uniform, unified.

einholen — to catch up (a person, vehicle, competitor etc. ahead of you). Also: to make up (lost time, a shortfall), to reel in (a net, flag etc.), to go shopping, to obtain, to get (information, permission, assistance etc.)
einig — united. Also: agreed, in agreement, single, only
einleiten — to commence, initiate, trigger, instigate, start.
einmalig — non-recurring, one-time, oncely. Also: singular, unique, unheard-of, second to none, unexcelled
einreichen — to send in, to hand in, to submit.
einschränken — to constrain. Also: to economize

einschätzen — to judge, to deem (the character or subjective classification of something, based on what information is available). Also: to estimate, to tell, to assess (the answer to a more open question, based on what information is available)
einsetzbar — usable, applicable.
eintauchen — to plunge. Also: to immerse, to dip, to steep
eintreffen — to arrive. Also: to come true

eintreten — to enter, to come into. Also: to occur, to eventuate, to join a club association etc. [with in (+ accusative)], to advocate, to support, to stand for, to champion [with für (+ accusative)], to kick [with auf (+ accusative)], to open (something e.g. a door) by kicking, to get something in one's foot by stepping on it [with accusative], to wear in (shoes)
einwerfen — to drop, to insert. Also: to smash by throwing, to interject (in a discussion), to pop (a pill, drugs etc.), to eat (indifferently, often fast food)
endgültig — final, ultimate. Also: definitive
entlassen — to release. Also: to dismiss, to fire, to sack (to dismiss without statutory notice period)

entnehmen — to take out, to remove (from a container or receptacle, into one's possession). Also: to take, to draw, to extract (from a source), to learn from, to conclude, to tell (a fact or conclusion from the observation or perusal of something)
entscheidend — decisive. Also: crucial
entsprechend — corresponding. Also: adequate, appropriate, respective
entstehend — originating, emerging, emerging. Also: nascent
enttäuschen — to disappoint (displease by not fulfilling an expectation).
erbauen — to build, erect. Also: to entertain, elevate, to edify

erfassen — to grasp, to grip, to seize (with one's hands). Also: to collide with, to crash into, to run over (especially of vehicles colliding with people, animals or movable objects), to catch, to hit (of spreading fires, avalanches, gusts of wind, etc. affecting an object), to realize, to conceive, to comprehend, to capture, to collect, to measure, to record (to add one or more data points to an existing aggregate data set, or to create a data set out of a property in general)
erfolgen — to happen, occur, take place. Also: to follow, ensue
erfolglos — unsuccessful.
erfordern — to necessitate, to require.
erfreuen — to please; to make happy. Also: to enjoy; to delight in

erheben — to raise, lift (one's arm, voice, glass, etc.). Also: to levy, charge, to gather, collect (e.g. data, material), to bring (charges), to rise (to one's feet, from one's seat, etc.), to rise (e.g. of a mountain or tower), to rise up, to feel superior
erhöhen — to heighten, raise. Also: to increase, to exalt
erkennbar — recognizable, discernible, visible, identifiable.
erkunden — to explore, to investigate.
ermitteln — to determine, to identify (a value or identity; through a calculation or other process). Also: to investigate (by the police)
erneut — renewed. Also: repeated

errichten — to build, to erect (a building or permanent structure, especially a large or individually significant one). Also: to erect, to set up (a barricade, bonfire, tent, or similar temporary arrangement; into a usable state), to establish, to form (a social order, regime, or similar system), to establish, to found (a foundation, an association)
erstmals — for the first time.
erstrecken — to extend. Also: to range, to cover
erweitern — to extend, to expand.
erwähnen — to mention (speak of something).
erzeugen — to produce. Also: to generate, to cause
erziehen — to nurture. Also: to educate
erzielen — to achieve, to obtain, to attain, to reach, to arrive, to realize. Also: to score (to earn points in a game)
ewig — eternal, everlasting. Also: constant, frequent
extern — external.
fehlerfrei — flawless. Also: sound, accurate

festhalten — to hold on (to) (to keep another object or person in place). Also: to hold on (to) [with an (+ dative) ‘to something’] (to keep oneself in place by gripping a fixture), to detain, to hold in custody (by force), to stick (to), to remain steadfast (in), to keep supporting, to keep (someone) on the team, to record, to capture (an event, e.g. through photo, video, or a chronicle), to clearly state, to say for the record
fließend — fluent (in language proficiency, etc.).
folglich — thus, therefore, consequently, hence.
fortsetzen — to continue with something.
frech — cheeky, barefaced, rude, saucy. Also: naughty

frieren — to be cold, feel cold [auxiliary haben] (unlike English freeze neither informal nor expressing extreme cold.). Also: to be cold, feel cold, to freeze, be freezing, be below 0 degrees celsius, synonym of gefrieren (“to freeze, become hard through cold”)
fälschlicherweise — wrongly, erroneously.
fügen — to join. Also: to add, to ordain, to submit, to comply
führend — leading, foremost.
gebrauchen — to use.
gebürtig — native, by birth (describing one's birth nationality, region, city, etc.).
geeignet — suitable, appropriate. Also: convenient
gefährden — to endanger, compromise.
gegenwärtig — current, contemporary, present, existing.
geheim — secret, clandestine. Also: familiar, dear, homely, cozy, tame, domesticated
geheimnisvoll — mysterious, cryptic, arcane, enigmatic. Also: secretive
gehoben — formal, elevated. Also: lofty, upscale
geistig — mental. Also: intellectual, spiritual
gelangen — to reach, get to, end up in (a place, location). Also: to reach, arrive at (a destination)
gelegen — situated. Also: convenient
gelegentlich — occasional.
geliebt — loved.
genannt — called, mentioned, named.
generell — general.
geplant — proposed, scheduled, planned.
geraten — to turn out, succeed. Also: to thrive, to come (across, by); to fall (upon), to get (into); to fall (into); to come (to); to fly (into)
gesamt — total, whole, entire, overall, big.
geschlossen — shut; closed; locked. Also: private, non-public, cohesive; solidary
geschäftlich — business, commercial.
gesellschaftlich — social (of or pertaining to society).
gesetzlich — lawful, legal, legitimate.
gewiss — certain.
gewünscht — desired, wanted. Also: requested
gleiten — to glide; to float; to move effortlessly. Also: to slide; to slip; to transition smoothly
gliedern — to structure, to organize, to divide. Also: to divide
glänzen — to shine, gleam, sparkle, glisten. Also: to be good, to stand out, to be noticeable
glücklicherweise — fortunately; luckily.
grob — coarse, rough. Also: uncouth, rude, crude, ill-defined
großartig — terrific, superb, etc..
gründlich — thorough, in-depth.
gönnen — to bestow, to indulge (someone's wishes), to treat to, to spoil. Also: to be happy for someone because of their good fortune
heilig — holy. Also: sacred
herausragend — outstanding. Also: prominent, jutting
hervorheben — to emphasize, highlight.
hervorragend — outstanding, superb. Also: paramount, eminent
hierzulande — in this country.
hilfreich — useful, helpful.
hinterlassen — to leave, to leave behind (after leaving a location). Also: to leave, to leave behind, to be survived by (after demise), to bequeath, to devise
hinweg — away. Also: over, across
hinweisen — to indicate. Also: to point out
hochwertig — high-end, premium, high-cost, high-grade, valuable, high-class, high-value, high-quality, worthy, upmarket. Also: Having a high valency
hässlich — ugly.
Höchst — Any of several towns in the German-speaking area.. Also: A district of Frankfurt-am-Main, Germany., a village in southern Hesse, Germany, a village in Vorarlberg, Austria
ignorieren — to ignore (deliberately pay no attention to).
indirekt — indirect. Also: implied, implicit, vicarious
industriell — industrial.
inhaltlich — content. Also: contextual, substantial (relating to substance)
innerhalb — within, inside (spatial). Also: within, in the space of (temporal)
insbesondere — in particular, particularly.
integrieren — to integrate.
interessiert — interested.
interpretieren — to interpret (to explain or tell the meaning of; to apprehend and represent by means of art). Also: to perform
jahrelang — longstanding. Also: yearslong; lasting for years
jobben — to have a job, to work (especially in a temporary or minor position).
keineswegs — in no way, by no means, not at all, not a bit.
klagen — to complain (to express feelings of pain, dissatisfaction, or resentment). Also: to wail, lament, to sue, to sue someone
kombinieren — to combine. Also: to deduct
kommend — coming, next. Also: next, future (holding a certain position in the future, especially after or just before the decision is made)
kommunistisch — communist, communistic.
komplex — complex, sophisticated, involved.
konkret — tangible. Also: specific, concrete (particular, perceivable, real)
kontaktieren — to contact (establish communication with).
koordinieren — to coordinate.
kostbar — precious. Also: sumptuous, magnificent, luxurious, splendid
kreisen — to circle, to wheel. Also: to revolve
kreuzen — to cross (place across). Also: to cross, intersect, to cross, pass over, to crossbreed
kühn — daring; audacious.
künftig — future.
künstlerisch — artistic.
künstlich — artificial.
laufend — current, ongoing, pending. Also: constant
lebendig — living, alive, animate. Also: lively, vivacious, spirited, animated, vibrant, active, agile
lediglich — merely.
lehnen — to lean. Also: to lean something
lehren — to teach (a class, a subject); to be a teacher. Also: to teach
leuchtend — luminous. Also: vibrant (of a colour); glowing, bright
locken — to lure, to entice, to tempt. Also: to be lured
luxuriös — luxurious.
ländlich — rural.
läuten — to ring, toll (a bell). Also: to ring, toll, to ring (at a door, front desk, etc.), to ring
malerisch — painting; painterly. Also: picturesque; scenic
mangelnd — lacking, wanting, insufficient.
manipulieren — to manipulate.
medizinisch — medicinal, medicated. Also: medical
mehrfach — several, multiple.
mehrheitlich — predominantly. Also: preponderantly
mehrmonatig — several-months-long; lasting several months.
mittelalterlich — medieval.
möglicherweise — possibly, potentially. Also: perhaps, conceivably
nachhaltig — long-term, sustainable, permanent, ongoing, lasting.
nachvollziehbar — understandable, comprehensible. Also: traceable
nachweisen — to prove, verify, establish, demonstrate (something or the presence of something), to substantiate, to provide proof of.
naturwissenschaftlich — scientific, science-oriented.

neigen — to incline, slant, tilt, bend, lower (move something in some direction by bowing or turning slightly). Also: to incline, slant, lean, bow (intransitive use always requires an adverb of place), to be inclined, to tend towards, to have a preference for
neulich — recently (in the recent past).
nicken — to nod (one's head).
niedrig — low.
nirgends — nowhere (in no place). Also: never (at no time)
nirgendwo — nowhere (in no place).
notwendig — necessary; essential; indispensable.
Null — zero (a numeral; numeric symbol of zero). Also: failure, loser
objektiv — objective (not influenced by emotions).
offensichtlich — obvious. Also: evident, apparent
optimieren — to optimize.
pendeln — to oscillate (move back and forth). Also: to commute across municipal boundaries
philosophisch — philosophical.
profitieren — to benefit. Also: to profit
provozieren — to provoke.
psychisch — psychical.
publizieren — to publish.

quasi — as it were, so to speak, effectively, essentially; used to mark a description as figurative, simplified or otherwise not to be taken as absolute, but illustrative of an important point. Also: as good as, basically, virtually, more or less; used to describe a process or change of state that has not been technically completed, but the remainder is considered minor or a mere formality
quälen — to torture, to torment, to agonize. Also: to cause sorrow, to upset, to worry, to harass, to bother, to toil, to struggle
ratsam — advisable.
recherchieren — to research, to investigate.
rechtlich — legal. Also: lawful
reduzieren — to reduce.
religiös — religious (concerning religion). Also: religious (committed to the practice of religion)
renommiert — renowned.
revolutionieren — to revolutionize.
runter — down, down here, downstairs (towards the speaker). Also: down, down there, downstairs (away from the speaker)
Rätoromanisch — Rhaeto-Romance (also referred to as die rätoromanische Sprache or die rätoromanischen Sprachen). Also: Romansch
sanft — gentle.
satt — not hungry, satiated, full, done. Also: fed up, sick of (see usage notes), full, ample
schlüpfen — to slip (move quickly). Also: to hatch
schmal — narrow, slender (not wide, comparatively long), small. Also: meagre, scarce
schweben — to hover, soar, float, drift.

schwingen — to swing (to move back and forth). Also: to move (somewhere) by swinging, to swing (to move something using a swinging motion), to wave; to brandish, to vibrate, to oscillate, to resound, to propagate (somewhere) as sound, to move throughout (an area) resounding, to be expressed (in someone's words), to ski back and forth, to arc, to clean (hemp or flax) of wood residues, to wrestle in the Schwingen (Swiss wrestling) style
schwitzen — to sweat; to perspire (of living beings). Also: to give off water (of things), to think hard; to work hard, to be in fear; to worry
sehnen — to yearn, to long.
seither — since then.
seltsam — strange, weird, odd, funny, curious. Also: rare, seldom
senken — to lower.
seriös — reputable, respectable, dignified; not shady or dubious; appearing civil, reliable, legitimate. Also: giving such an impression, thus formal, elegant
sichtbar — visible.
signalisieren — to signal.
sinnlos — meaningless, pointless, senseless. Also: excessive, disproportionate
slawisch — Slavic, Slav, Slavonic.
Sorbisch — Sorbian (belonging or relating to the Sorbian people). Also: Sorbian (in or relating to the Sorbian language)
sozusagen — so to speak, in a manner of speaking.
spiegeln — to mirror.
staatlich — state, government. Also: state-owned
statistisch — statistical.
stattdessen — instead, in lieu (of).
steigend — rising. Also: increasing
steigern — to increase, to raise. Also: to boost, to improve, to enhance, to compare
stetig — steady, continuous (without break, cessation, or interruption in time).

steuern — to steer, to navigate, to pilot, to operate, to control. Also: to govern, to direct, to head; to control, [with auf (+ accusative)] to use something or someone for support, to pay a tax, to levy from, to tax, [with dative] to contravene, to withsit, [with dative] to assist, to come to the aid of
strömen — to flow, stream, pour.
ständig — permanent, constant (without breaks). Also: persistent, incessant, constant (repeated very often)
sächsisch — Saxon (of, from or relating to Saxony in modern Germany). Also: Saxon; Upper Saxon (pertaining the High German dialect spoken in Saxony), Saxon (of the old Saxon tribe and their language)
tagelang — lasting for days.
tagsüber — by day, during the day, in the day, in the daytime.
technisch — technical. Also: technological, engineering
teils — partly, partially.
thematisieren — to bring up, address an issue.
theoretisch — theoretical.
tierisch — animal; beastly, brutal. Also: extremely
traumhaft — dreamlike, belonging in a dream. Also: dreamy, attractive, gorgeous, amazing
trösten — to console, to comfort, to support someone (emotionally).
umbringen — to kill, to murder, to do away with. Also: to commit suicide
umdrehen — to twist, to turn, to turn around. Also: to turn one's head and look back, to turn inside out (clothing, etc.), to turn around, to flip, to reverse
umsetzen — to implement, to realize. Also: to transform, translate, convert, transpose
umweltfreundlich — environmentally friendly.
unbemerkt — unnoticed, unseen, unobserved.
unbezahlt — unpaid.
unentgeltlich — not in return for payment. Also: not to purchase, not for valuable consideration
ungehindert — unhindered. Also: unimpeded
ungern — unwillingly; usually expressed in English through verb phrases such as "doesn't like", "don't want", etc..
unklar — unclear.
unterbreiten — to submit (e.g. a plan for consideration), to suggest.
unverändert — unchanged, unaltered, unmodified.
unzählig — countless, innumerable.
verankern — to anchor. Also: to enshrine (to protect within an official law or treaty)
veranstalten — to organize, to arrange, to stage (a public or social event).
verbergen — to hide, conceal from view, keep secret.
verdrängen — to displace. Also: to replace, supersede, oust, to repress, suppress
vereinen — to unite. Also: to join (together), to combine
vereinfachen — to simplify (to make simpler). Also: to simplify (to become simpler)
verhandeln — to negotiate, to debate. Also: to bargain, to try (a court case), to sell
verknüpfen — to combine.

verleihen — to award (someone an honor, a medal, etc); to confer (a title or degree upon someone). Also: to lend (something to someone, for a finite period of time), to vest (a power in someone), to give (a right to someone), to give (an attribute to something)
vernünftig — reasonable, sensible.

verpflichten — to oblige someone, make someone promise. Also: to commit, to oblige, to oblige, to swear, to be sworn, to engage, to bind someone by contract, to oblige someone, to be an obligation, to undertake (to commit oneself to an obligation)
verschlechtern — to deteriorate (make worse). Also: to worsen; to make worse
verschwunden — disappeared.
versenden — to ship, to dispatch.
verstorben — deceased, late.
verstärken — to reinforce. Also: to amplify, to penetrate, to grow stronger
vertiefen — to deepen. Also: to delve
verzweifelt — desperate.
verändert — changed, altered.
verärgern — to annoy, to anger, to upset. Also: to antagonize
veröffentlicht — published.
vielfach — multiple, manifold.
vielfältig — diverse, varied. Also: manifold
virtuell — virtual (in effect; not fact).
vollkommen — perfect (fitting its definition precisely). Also: complete
vorhanden — available, existing.
vorkommen — to occur, happen. Also: to seem, appear, to appear, to occur (in a given location or situation), to feel (smart, stupid, clever, dumb, etc.)
vorlegen — to present, to submit. Also: to serve (food) to put down, put out (cat food etc.), to put on (a chain, a latch), to put (a bolt) across, to advance
vorschreiben — to prescribe, to dictate, to command.
vorweisen — to show. Also: to perform
wahr — true, real. Also: true, truthful
wahrnehmen — to sense, to perceive, to apprehend, to notice. Also: to keep or make it to an appointment, to use; to take advantage of, to assume
wehren — to defend oneself. Also: to prevent the spread of something, to fight, to deny, to prevent someone from doing something
weichen — to move farther away, to make room, to yield. Also: to wane, to decrease
weitergeben — to impart (to communicate the knowledge of). Also: to pass on, to share
weiterleiten — to forward. Also: to redirect
werben — to recruit; to enlist. Also: to promote, advertise, to attract, to woo
wesentlich — essential (concerning the essence or true nature of something). Also: crucial, principal, significant (of fundamental importance)
westlich — west, western.
wetten — to bet. Also: to bet, guess (that something is the case with certainty)

wiedergeben — to give back, to return (something to its rightful or previous owner). Also: to render, to echo, to reproduce (to state or represent the content of some source or conversation), to play (of audio or video data)
wirtschaftlich — economical. Also: efficient
wärmen — to warm.
würzig — spicy.
wütend — furious. Also: angry
zeitgenössisch — contemporary (of the same age as something). Also: contemporary (of the present age; especially of art or culture)
zunehmend — increasing, growing, mounting.
zurechtfinden — to find one's way, to get along.
zurückführen — to restore or return. Also: to repatriate, to ascribe, to attribute
zurücktreten — to step back. Also: to resign, to step down, to rescind, to cancel
zusagen — to accept, to promise to come. Also: to pledge, to promise, to please; to appeal to
zusammenfassen — to summarize, to sum up. Also: to consolidate
zuschauen — to look on. Also: to watch
zusätzlich — additional, extra, supplementary. Also: ancillary
zwangsläufig — compulsory, inevitable.
zweifeln — to doubt, to be doubtful.
zweisprachig — bilingual. Also: containing text in two languages, able to communicate in two languages
zwingend — compelling, mandatory. Also: necessary

zwischendurch — between times, in between (during some period of time, dividing it into intervals). Also: in the meanwhile, interim (during an intermediate period), now and then, on the side (occasionally when there is time to spare), in between, right in/through the middle
Änderung — change, modification.
Äußerung — utterance. Also: statement
Öffentlichkeitsarbeit — public relations (profession). Also: outreach
Öffnung — opening. Also: orifice
Übernachtung — overnight stay (at a hotel etc.). Also: sleepover
Überschrift — heading. Also: headline
Überschwemmung — flooding; flood; overflow of a body of water.
Überwachung — surveillance, monitoring.
ähneln — [with dative] to resemble, be or look similar to.
äußerst — utmostly, extremely. Also: as the last and least favoured possibility
örtlich — local.
östlich — east, eastern.
übergeben — to hand over. Also: to vomit, to throw up
überraschend — surprising.
übersichtlich — easily looked over and understood; clear; overseeable. Also: well arranged, clearly arranged, small, meager, sparse

übertragen — to transfer, transmit. Also: to translate, to broadcast, televise, to hand over something to someone, entrust someone with something, confer a right on someone, to have a post-term birth, to be passed on to someone (of a disease or illness), to communicate itself to someone (of enthusiasm, nervousness, etc.)
überwinden — to overcome, to get over.

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
